topic: Someone receives a package intended for the previous tenant
raw_output:
[story 1]

The delivery notice was flimsy, taped haphazardly to my doorframe. It was addressed to 'A. Jenkins, Apartment 3B.' I stared at the plain brown box sitting on my welcome mat, the tape looking suspiciously new. I picked it up, the cardboard cool and slightly damp beneath my fingertips. My brow furrowed as I examined the return address—a name I didn't recognize, a number that didn't match any building directory I'd seen. I tapped the package twice with my knuckle, a sharp, uneven sound in the quiet hallway. I glanced toward the apartment door, a flicker of unease tightening my chest. Why would someone send something to me? I let out a slow breath, trying to keep my expression neutral, but my gaze kept drifting back to the unfamiliar label.

[story 2]

I found the package tucked behind the recycling bin, a dark rectangle nestled amongst flattened cardboard. The sender's name was scrawled in hurried, looping handwriting. I pulled it out, my fingers brushing against the slightly rough texture of the wrapping paper. It felt too deliberate, too pointed. I carried it into my living room, setting it on the coffee table, and circled it slowly, my eyes scanning every seam and crease. A knot formed in my stomach, a dull, heavy pressure that made my jaw clench. I reread the address—a previous tenant’s details. I picked up my phone, intending to call building management, but the words caught in my throat. What was the point of it? I just shook my head, the motion jerky, and placed the box back down, resolving to ignore the oddity.

[story 3]

The sound of the delivery notification jarred me out of my morning routine. A package. It was addressed to the unit above mine. I approached the door cautiously, my hand hovering near the deadbolt. When I finally opened it, the package sat there, unassuming, yet somehow radiating an unwarranted sense of intrusion. I took it inside, my movements stiff. I examined the contents without opening it, just observing the shape and weight. A low murmur escaped my lips, a sound more of disbelief than acknowledgment. I walked over to the kitchen counter, leaning against it, my posture rigid. I spent several minutes just staring at the box, trying to find some logical explanation for why this item had ended up in my hands. It felt like a deliberate misdirection, and I wasn't about to let it derail my day with unfounded worries.
